mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hike periods with p-value < 0.05, or Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in opposite direction.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns remain positive in over 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.3pp, or signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand strength, driving corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day forward returns in > 60% of cases, or interaction term Rank IC is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during periods of high inflation indicates supply-side bottlenecks that compress profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth during high inflation periods yields positive 20-day forward returns in > 50% of cases, or regime-specific Rank IC falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Higher core inflation readings force markets to price in prolonged restrictive monetary policy, increasing equity risk premiums and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of high CPIAUCSL observations, or rolling Rank IC remains negative over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payrolls growth combined with a stable unemployment rate reflects robust labor market absorption, supporting consumer spending and yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and stable UNRATE produces negative 60-day forward returns in > 55% of instances, or interaction effect Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth during late-cycle expansions signals overheating economic activity that prompts central bank tightening expectations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth in late-cycle regimes yields positive 20-day forward returns in > 60% of cases, or regime-conditional Rank IC is not significantly different from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production directly correlates with near-term corporate revenue growth and positive earnings revisions, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of periods with rising INDPRO, or walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 across all test windows.
